6.anchor: Fifteen days before the Festival of Shelters began,
6.note: Hebrew On the first day of the seventh month.

7.anchor: Then the people hired masons and carpenters and bought cedar logs from the people of Tyre and Sidon, paying them with food, wine, and olive oil. The logs were brought down from the Lebanon mountains and floated along the coast of the Mediterranean Sea

8.anchor: The construction of the Temple of God began in midspring,

9.anchor: The workers at the Temple of God were supervised by Jeshua with his sons and relatives, and Kadmiel and his sons, all descendants of Hodaviah.
9.note: Hebrew sons of Judah bene Yehudah Bene Yehudah

2.anchor: Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and Jeshua son of Jehozadak

3.anchor: But Tattenai, governor of the province west of the Euphrates River,
3.note: Aramaic the province beyond the river;

4.note: As in one Hebrew manuscript and Greek and Syriac versions; Masoretic Text reads Then we told them.

12.anchor: But because our ancestors angered the God of heaven, he abandoned them to King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon,

13.note: King Cyrus of Persia is here identified as the king of Babylon because Persia had conquered the Babylonian Empire.

5.anchor: They bribed agents to work against them and to frustrate their plans. This went on during the entire reign of King Cyrus of Persia and lasted until King Darius of Persia took the throne.

7.anchor: Even later, during the reign of King Artaxerxes of Persia,

10.anchor: They also sent greetings from the rest of the people whom the great and noble Ashurbanipal
had deported and relocated in Samaria and throughout the neighboring lands of the province west of the Euphrates River.
10.note: Aramaic the province beyond the river;

14.note: Aramaic Since we eat the salt of the palace.

24.anchor: So the work on the Temple of God in Jerusalem had stopped, and it remained at a standstill until the second year of the reign of King Darius of Persia.
24.note: The second year of Darius’s reign was 520 b.c.
As in parallel text at Neh 7:28; Hebrew reads Azmaveth.

25.note: As in some Hebrew manuscripts and Greek version (see also Neh 7:29); Hebrew reads Kiriath-arim.

46.note: As in an alternate reading of the Masoretic Text (see also Neh 7:48); the other alternate reads Shamlai.

69.anchor: and each leader gave as much as he could. The total of their gifts came to 61,000 gold coins,

1.anchor: In the first year of King Cyrus of Persia,
1.note: The first year of Cyrus’s reign over Babylon was 538 b.c.

1.anchor: fulfilled the prophecy he had given through Jeremiah.

8.anchor: Cyrus directed Mithredath, the treasurer of Persia, to count these items and present them to Sheshbazzar, the leader of the exiles returning to Judah.
8.note: Hebrew Sheshbazzar, the prince of Judah.

9.note: The meaning of this Hebrew word is uncertain.

6.anchor: Then Ezra left the front of the Temple of God and went to the room of Jehohanan son of Eliashib. He spent the night
6.note: As in parallel text at 1 Esdras 9:2; Hebrew reads He went.

9.anchor: Within three days, all the people of Judah and Benjamin had gathered in Jerusalem. This took place on December 19,
9.note: Hebrew on the twentieth day of the ninth month,

16.anchor: So this was the plan they followed. Ezra selected leaders to represent their families, designating each of the representatives by name. On December 29,
16.note: Hebrew On the first day of the tenth month,

17.anchor: By March 27, the first day of the new year,
17.note: Hebrew By the first day of the first month,

18.anchor: From the family of Jeshua son of Jehozadak

25.anchor: From the family of Parosh: Ramiah, Izziah, Malkijah, Mijamin, Eleazar, Hashabiah,
25.note: As in parallel text at 1 Esdras 9:26; Hebrew reads Malkijah.

37.note: As in Greek version; Hebrew reads Jaasu, Bani, Binnui.

44.anchor: Each of these men had a pagan wife, and some even had children by these wives.
44.note: Or and they sent them away with their children.

3.anchor: “Let the Temple be rebuilt on the site where Jews used to offer their sacrifices, using the original foundations. Its height will be ninety feet, and its width will be ninety feet.
3.note: Aramaic Its height will be 60 cubits and its width will be 60 cubits.

6.anchor: “Now therefore, Tattenai, governor of the province west of the Euphrates River,
6.note: Aramaic the province beyond the river;

11.anchor: “Those who violate this decree in any way will have a beam pulled from their house. Then they will be lifted up and impaled on it, and their house will be reduced to a pile of rubble.

15.anchor: The Temple was completed on March 12,
15.note: Aramaic on the third day of the month Adar,

19.note: Hebrew On the fourteenth day of the first month,

22.anchor: had caused the king of Assyria
22.note: King Darius of Persia is here identified as the king of Assyria because Persia had conquered the Babylonian Empire, which included the earlier Assyrian Empire.

1.anchor: Many years later, during the reign of King Artaxerxes of Persia,
there was a man named Ezra. He was the son

3.anchor: son of Amariah, son of Azariah, son

5.anchor: son of Abishua, son of Phinehas, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the high priest.

8.anchor: Ezra arrived in Jerusalem in August

9.anchor: He had arranged to leave Babylon on April 8, the first day of the new year,
9.note: Hebrew on the first day of the first month,

9.anchor: and he arrived at Jerusalem on August 4,
9.note: Hebrew on the first day of the fifth month,

21.anchor: “I, Artaxerxes the king, hereby send this decree to all the treasurers in the province west of the Euphrates River
21.note: Aramaic the province beyond the river;

22.anchor: You are to give him up to 7,500 pounds
of wheat, 550 gallons of wine, 550 gallons of olive oil,
22.note: Aramaic 100 baths of wine, 100 baths of olive oil.

5.note: As in some Greek manuscripts (see also 1 Esdras 8:32); Hebrew lacks Zattu.

10.note: As in some Greek manuscripts (see also 1 Esdras 8:36); Hebrew lacks Bani.

13.anchor: From the family of Adonikam, who came later
13.note: Or who were the last of his family.

14.anchor: From the family of Bigvai: Uthai, Zaccur,
14.note: As in Greek and Syriac versions and an alternate reading of the Masoretic Text; the other alternate reads Zabbud.

18.anchor: Since the gracious hand of our God was on us, they sent us a man named Sherebiah, along with eighteen of his sons and brothers. He was a very astute man and a descendant of Mahli, who was a descendant of Levi son of Israel.
18.note: is the name that God gave to Jacob. Israel

22.anchor: For I was ashamed to ask the king for soldiers and horsemen

27.anchor: 20 gold bowls, equal in value to 1,000 gold coins,

31.anchor: We broke camp at the Ahava Canal on April 19
31.note: Hebrew on the twelfth day of the first month,

36.anchor: The king’s decrees were delivered to his highest officers and the governors of the province west of the Euphrates River,
36.note: Hebrew the province beyond the river.
